title: fremontia californica subsp. crassifolia
intro: Fremontia californica subsp. crassifolia, also known as the California fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family native to the southwestern United States and Baja California. The California fleabane has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. Its health benefits include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Fremontia californica subsp. crassifolia can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile and valuable plant in natural remedies.
